title: Phenomenon 17
wikitext:

{{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 17: Roaming Levels}}__NOTOC__{{Phenomenon Class|frequency=3|effectiveness=5|danger=4}}[[File:LEVELS.png|thumb|The three most common types of intersection.|578x578px]] ==OVERVIEW== "Roaming Levels" or Phenomenon 17 refers to levels that are not physically tied to any V-Location (Void Location) like other levels. As a result of this detachment from their V-Location, these levels have a tendency to wander from where they were and intersect with other levels. When a Roaming Level intersects a stationary level or another Roaming level, Wanderers who are in the area of the intersection are more susceptible to being no-clipped to the Roaming Level. Once inside, the chances of no-clipping back into the stationary level are low due to the speed exhibited by Roaming Levels when moving through the Void. Currently, the reason for levels to become Roaming are currently unknown, so is the amount of Roaming Levels currently active. As of writing, 5 Roaming Levels have been recorded by Wanderers, but due to the standards set by the M.E.G, B.L.S.A.I.D, and E.S.I.S.P.S Archive Committee when it comes to authorizing new levels, they have not be entered into the official Backrooms Archive Database for public distribution. ==SCIENTIFIC DESCRIPTION== While encounters with Roaming Levels are low, the science behind them is very well understood. When a level is created it creates a tether to an entropic area of the universe. This area has been solely dedicated to the housing of levels within the Backrooms by unknown means and is known as The Bedrock. Theoretically, the Bedrock would be accessed via the Void, however, due to the properties of the Void, attempts to access the Bedrock have been unsuccessful. When a level is tethered, it becomes subject to the [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Law]],gains said attributes, and is physically tethered to that area of the Bedrock. This physicality is also what allows for [[No-Clipping|No]]-[[Phenomenon 1-A|Clipping]]. The area dedicated to that level is known as their V-Location or Void Location since the level physically manifests in the Void while being tethered to the Bedrock. However, by currently unknown means, levels can be severed from their attachment to the Bedrock, and therefore their V-Location, as well as their susceptibility to the Casper-Bray Law. It is at this point that the level begins to roam and can intersect other levels. As of writing, there are only 3 types of intersections; * Lifts, which involve the Roaming Level intersecting upwards. This is the intersection with the highest probability of carrying wanderers with it. * Pass-Bys, which involve the Roaming Level intersected horizontally at a corner. This type of intersection has only been recorded once during the R-3 Incident. * Total Intersections, which involve the Roaming Level totally intersecting and eventually overlapping the original level. This type has the potential to carry off entire outposts and populations should one occur. ==HISTORY== The first possible recorded instance of a Roaming Level was during the M.E.G 500 Expedition Project in 54 BY (Back-Years, 2017 in the Frontrooms). While unconfirmed due to the nature of the Roaming Level, recorded footage, journal entries, and testimony match the known attributes of a Roaming Level. [[File:747.jpg|thumb|According to testimony of the surviving Jackrabbit members, this is where the intersection occurred.|374x374px]] After entry into [[Level 402]] by Expeditionary Group A (Jackrabbits), the first Roaming Level (R-1), intersected with half of the land mass of the level, picking up 17 members of the group and carrying them with the level. This was only noticed by the 17 members after R-1 fully left Level 402, leaving them no way back. After the R-1 disconnected, digital and radio communication with Jackrabbits was cut off. The 17 members were initially declared dead due to an entity attack, and the 3 remaining members of Jackrabbits stayed on 402 until Expeditionary Group B (Coney) was sent to continue the effort and reintegrated the 3 members. In 59 BY (2022), during construction of an M.E.G Outpost on 402 as part of the Humanization Project, 11 of the 17 missing members of Jackrabbit suddenly manifested 22 feet above the ocean to the west of the outpost. M.E.G forces quickly recovered the members and treated them for a variety of cuts, bruises, burns, amputations, and frostbite injuries. As a result of their recovery, they were sent to Base Alpha for a series of hearings about their experience, those records can be accessed at the end of this article. ---- Since then, recorded instances of Roaming Levels are rare, with only 4 additional distinct Roaming Levels having been identified due to a series of incidents. We will cover them below.
'''> Open Incident Overview; R-2'''
'''> Close Incident Overview; R-2'''
{{Class 5|header=Theorized Threat Class:|class=5|safety=Likely Unsafe|security=Reportedly Unsecure|entity=Reportedly Entity Infested|hexagon=5?}} ==INCIDENT OVERVIEW:== During renovation of Base Alpha's Entity Containment Research Wing in 55 BY (2018), a Roaming Level intersected with the Smiler Research Containment Unit, causing the crew of 5 construction workers to demainfest from the unit and into R-2. Afterward, the Unit was discovered coated in a thick viscous slime and dotted with small red orbs. In 56 BY (2019), 3 of the 5 workers manifested above the memorial meant for the workers after the incident. 1 died upon impact with the memorial and 2 sustained minor injuries. They went on to give testimony to the Roaming Level Research Board. Since this incident, attempts to enter R-2 have not been successful. A tripod camera has been placed within the Containment Unit in hopes that it is teleported into R-2 should it intersect again. ==ROAMING LEVEL DESCRIPTION:== According to the 2 workers who survived, R-2 is a large abandoned nature reserve covered in the same slime that was recovered in the Smiler Containment Unit. This slime has entirely encased all flora and fauna within R-2, except for a series of entities that the workers described as Goos. The only areas absent of the slime are 2 man made buildings present in the northern section of the reserve that acted as a gift-shop and management building. During the duration of their stay within R-2, the workers stayed within both the Gift-Shop and management building as they reported that the two buildings appeared to stay in perfect shape despite the outside condition and that the gift-shop constantly manifested new food and water every 24 hours. During their stay, the workers reported a series of entities known as Goos, which were described as "spherical entities made out of slime with a hard and indestructible center". While it is currently unknown how many "Goos" there are, the workers encountered 3 different types. * Drone Goos, which hovered above the nature reserve and occasionally sent large rays of green spores down towards the ground. If someone gets caught in the wave of spores, they are infected with a unknown fungus and die within 24 hours according to reports from the workers * Hunter Goos, which only came out during the night and roamed around the forest within the nature reserve. Due to the hostile nature of his entity and the circumstance of how the entity was encountered, the exact description of the entity is not particularly clear. However, reports indicate that Hunter Goos consisted on one large spherical mass with 3 large spikes of hard slime projecting from the center. They are able to clear large distances of ground within seconds, and can seemingly jump very high, though the exact measurements of either are unknown. * Mother Goo. The day before R-2 intersected with the Memorial, the workers reportedly angered a very large goo entity that was only referred to as the Mother Goo. There are no descriptions of the Mother Goo, as the only worker that perceived the entity and made it out died upon remanifestation to the memorial. The other surviving workers describe the entity as having a loud rumbling roar with a lack of sound, and seemingly the size of the building they were housed in. ==OUTCOME== Due to the nature of the testimony about R-2, and the entities within, further purposeful experimentation with finding R-2 has been cancelled.
'''> Open Incident Overview; R-3'''
'''> Close Incident Overview; R-3'''
{{Class 3|hexagon=3?|header=Theoretical Threat Level: 3|class=3|safety=Likely Safe|security=Reportedly Securable|entity=Medium Entity Population}} [[File:Container-yard-1-1531015.jpg|thumb|Only picture of R-3. Taken upon entry.|322x322px]] ==INCIDENT OVERVIEW== During transport of wheat from Level 10 to Level 11 for experimentation in M.E.G Agricultural Outpost 17 in 57 BY (2020), 2 Couriers who were transporting a large amount of the wheat in Void Bags were suddenly manifested into R-3 during a Lift intersection event. When the rest of the couriers noticed their sudden disappearance, a large shipping container suddenly manifested and fell several feet away. 2 BY later (2022), 1 of the couriers manifested in [[Level 11.11]] inside the Colonist Cash-Dew Outpost, alongside 2 shipping containers, which killed 3 other people, but the courier survived. The other courier manifested 1 BY (2023) later in a large, ocean-sized, pool in [[Level 37.1]] afloat on a shipping container with several hundred loafs of bread in their void bag. Both were recovered and sent to Base Alpha. ==ROAMING LEVEL DESCRIPTION:== As of writing, R-3 is the only Roaming Level that has a picture taken of it, and extensive description from both couriers involved. According to both couriers, R-3 is a large container and shipping yard approximately 4067.39 KM in size at a minimum, and is entirely covered by shipping containers, paths for walking, beached cargo ships, or large monolithic buildings with no available entrance. There is no flora or fauna within R-3, however, according to the couriers, placing raw material within the containers, closing the doors, and opening them 24 hours later results in them turning into a refined version. The couriers used this to turn the wheat they carried into bread. While no entities roam R-3 actively, one entity reportedly protects the tall buildings within R-3, and was only spotted on the last day within R-3 before reappearance within their respective levels. Neither of the couriers got a picture or good look at the entity, but did vaguely described the entity as a "large, about the size of a couple of cargo containers, spider-like entity made of concrete and rebar." ==OUTCOME== Due to the properties exhibited by the containers within R-3, the M.E.G Resource Exploitation Committee considers gaining access to R-3 a priority for large scale food and component creation. Attempts to gain access, though, have yet to succeed due to the nature of roaming levels.
'''> Open Incident Overview R-4'''
'''> Close Incident Overview R-4'''
{{Class 3e|}} [[File:BurntForest77.jpg|left|thumb|Photo of R-4's Ash Forest|323x323px]] ==INCIDENT OVERVIEW== During transport of timber from [[Level 45]] to [[Level 154]] for processing in 58 BY (2021), 4 security guards were lured towards a den of Skin-Stealers by an infantile Skin-Stealer practicing lures in [[Level 65]]. When the team figured this out, they began to flee, which is when R-4 intersected via a Pass-By, which took the whole group. 4 BY later (2025), 2 of the security guards manifested in the middle of Base Alpha, one with a amputated right arm, and the other with several cuts across his body. 2 weeks later, the other two security guards manifested in Level 154 inside a train car. At the same time as they manifested, the Hermes Drives of 5 separate Neon Express trains disappeared. Both were transported to Base Alpha. ==ROAMING LEVEL DESCRIPTION:== [[File:FoundryR4.jpg|thumb|Image from within R-4's Manufacturing Hub|402x402px]] R-4 is 4834.15 km in size, though reports indicate that it does go further, and is composed of three distinct regions; * The Ash Forest; a large, several hundred kilometers in size, burnt forest. The air is ashy and, while not toxic to breathe, is described as "tough to breathe", and led to one of the security guards developing lung cancer. Despite the lack of entities, the Ash Forest is home to "burn-ups", large sudden bursts of flame that come from tunnels in the ground and last for hours. * The Foundry; composes the rest of the space recorded to belong to R-4, and is, as the name suggests, a large foundry building. Despite the lack of entities or visible automation to run the foundry, the foundry continues to operate at a above average pace for a facility like it, and it is prone to accidents, as one occurred during the R-4 incident. It is too hot in the foundry to safely sleep or live, but the presence of Ash Forests within Foundries allowed for the team to sleep cautiously within them * ???; During the team's last day within R-4, they uncovered a region that was neither the Forest nor the Foundry that consisted of a train station that led across a large body of water equivalent to that of an ocean. Upon all members boarding the train, it proceeded to move down the line towards a separate land mass populated by large spikes. However, before they could reach the land mass, they were deposited in their respective places. ==OUTCOME:== Research into R-4 is heavily classified due to its potential uses. Further information has been classified.
---- '''END OF FILE'''
> what.

UNRECOGNIZED COMMAND

OPENING...

> open stream for user: Head of the Committee of Roaming Levels

> Yes, Last? How is the file?

> I'm sorry, but you don't have the permissions for those, Last.

> i will get first on your ass so help me.

> your family isn't.

> Don't joke.

> Fine. I'll send it to your terminal. Don't expect anything from me again.

> i don't want anything else from your anyway. waste.
'''> Open R-1/5'''
'''> Close R-1/5'''
''What great things have you done?''
''Pierce the heavens and the ground.''
''But you are not whole.''
WARNING: THIS FILE IS REGARDING THE ⌘ INVESTIGATION. ANY UNAUTHORIZED VIEWING WILL BE MET WITH IMPRISONMENT BY M.E.G SECURITY FORCES
==DESCRIPTION== [[File:1603871.jpg|thumb|The sky of R-1 with distant structures in the background. Not to be reached.|378x378px]] R-1, also identified as R-5, is a Roaming Level currently stuck within the center of First's residence within Base Alpha. R-1 is composed of a piece of Bedrock that has since been severed from the Bedrock as a whole and drifted to the First's residence. The current size of R-1 is currently unknown, as previous exploration parties have yet to find a limit to the level. All current exploratory and research parties into R-1 have been cancelled due to the discovery of R-1's primary feature, a large Stele that juts out from the ground approximately 77 miles away from the current entrance of R-1. Upon entering a 10-mile range of the Stele, the sky and surroundings begin to mimic that of an outdoor park. This is only a visual change, however, as benches located around the Stele are not physical, nor is anything else except the Stele. Below is a transcript of the Stele ==STELE TRANSCRIPT==
''O Father O Father''
''He left us here to die''
''And we lived all we could''
''And we burned all we wanted''
''And we cared for his people''
''And yet he did not return''
''Now we are left with our spent lives''
''Now we are left with our ashes''
''Now we are left with his mistakes''
''Like you, First''
''You will be the first thing we give to him as penance''
''And you will be the last thing we see before our ascendance''
''You, You, You''


''Lovingly crafted by Signs-in-the-Mist''
